Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. a) Find the cross product $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ of the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express the answer in component form. b) Sketch the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 2 , 0 , 0 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 2 , 2 , 0 \rangle$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. a) Find the cross product $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ of the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express the answer in component form. b) Sketch the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 3 , 2 , -1 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 1 , 1 , 0 \rangle$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. a) Find the cross product $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ of the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express the answer in component form. b) Sketch the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = 2 \mathbf{\text{i}} + 3 \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \mathbf{\text{j}} + 2 \mathbf{\text{k}}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. a) Find the cross product $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ of the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express the answer in component form. b) Sketch the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = 2 \mathbf{\text{j}} + 3 \mathbf{\text{k}} ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = 3 \mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{k}}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. a) Find the cross product $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ of the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express the answer in component form. b) Sketch the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Simplify $$( \mathbf{\text{i}} \times \mathbf{\text{i}} - 2 \mathbf{\text{i}} \times \mathbf{\text{j}} - 4 \mathbf{\text{i}} \times \mathbf{\text{k}} + 3 \mathbf{\text{j}} \times \mathbf{\text{k}} ) \times \mathbf{\text{i}} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. a) Find the cross product $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ of the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express the answer in component form. b) Sketch the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Simplify $$\mathbf{\text{j}} \times ( \mathbf{\text{k}} \times \mathbf{\text{j}} + 2 \mathbf{\text{j}} \times \mathbf{\text{i}} - 3 \mathbf{\text{j}} \times \mathbf{\text{j}} + 5 \mathbf{\text{i}} \times \mathbf{\text{k}} ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 3 , -1 , 2 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle -2 , 0 , 1 \rangle$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 2 , 6 , 1 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 3 , 0 , 1 \rangle$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \vec{A B} ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \vec{A C} ,$$ where $$A ( 1 , 0 , 1 ) ,$$ $$B ( 1 , -1 , 3 ) ,$$ and $$C ( 0 , 0 , 5 )$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \vec{O P} ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \vec{P Q} ,$$ where $$P ( -1 , 1 , 0 )$$ and $$Q ( 0 , 2 , 1 )$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: Determine the real number $$\alpha$$ such that $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{i}}$$ are orthogonal, where $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = 3 \mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{j}} - 5 \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = 4 \mathbf{\text{i}} - 2 \mathbf{\text{j}} + \alpha \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: Show that $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ and $$2 \mathbf{\text{i}} - 14 \mathbf{\text{j}} + 2 \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ cannot be orthogonal for any $$\alpha$$ real number, where $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \mathbf{\text{i}} + 7 \mathbf{\text{j}} - \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \alpha \mathbf{\text{i}} + 5 \mathbf{\text{j}} + \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: Show that $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ is orthogonal to $$\mathbf{\text{u}} + \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} - \mathbf{\text{v}} ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are nonzero vectors.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: Show that $$\mathbf{\text{v}} \times \mathbf{\text{u}}$$ is orthogonal to $$\left(\right. \text{u} \cdot \text{v} \left.\right) \left(\right. \text{u} + \text{v} \left.\right) + \textbf{u} ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are nonzero vectors.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: Calculate the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}\mathbf{\text{i}} & \mathbf{\text{j}} & \mathbf{\text{k}} \\ 1 & -1 & 7 \\ 2 & 0 & 3\end{matrix}\right | .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Find unit vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ in the direction of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Express your answer using standard unit vectors.
Question: Calculate the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}\mathbf{\text{i}} & \mathbf{\text{j}} & \mathbf{\text{k}} \\ 0 & 3 & -4 \\ 1 & 6 & -1\end{matrix}\right | .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle -1 , 0 , e^{t} \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 1 , e^{\text{unicode[:-]} t} , 0 \rangle ,$$ where $$t$$ is a real number

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 1 , 0 , x \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle \frac{2}{x} , 1 , 0 \rangle ,$$ where $$x$$ is a nonzero real number

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Find vector $$\left(\right. \text{a} - 2 \textbf{b} \left.\right) \times \textbf{c} ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{a}} = \left |\begin{matrix}\mathbf{\text{i}} & \mathbf{\text{j}} & \mathbf{\text{k}} \\ 2 & -1 & 5 \\ 0 & 1 & 8\end{matrix}\right | ,$$ $$\textbf{b} = \left|\right. \textbf{i} & \textbf{j} & \textbf{k} \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & -2 \left|\right. ,$$ and $$\textbf{c} = \text{i} + \text{j} + \textbf{k} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Find vector $$\textbf{c} \times \left(\right. \text{a} + 3 \textbf{b} \left.\right) ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{a}} = \left |\begin{matrix}\mathbf{\text{i}} & \mathbf{\text{j}} & \mathbf{\text{k}} \\ 5 & 0 & 9 \\ 0 & 1 & 0\end{matrix}\right | ,$$ $$\textbf{b} = \left|\right. \text{i} & \text{j} & \text{k} \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \\ 7 & 1 & -1 \left|\right. ,$$ and $$\textbf{c} = \text{i} - \text{k} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: [T] Use the cross product $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ to find the acute angle between vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \mathbf{\text{i}} + 2 \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$ Express the answer in degrees rounded to the nearest integer.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: [T] Use the cross product $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ to find the obtuse angle between vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \text{unicode[:-]} \mathbf{\text{i}} + 3 \mathbf{\text{j}} + \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \mathbf{\text{i}} - 2 \mathbf{\text{j}} .$$ Express the answer in degrees rounded to the nearest integer.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Use the sine and cosine of the angle between two nonzero vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ to prove Lagrange’s identity: $$\Vert \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} \Vert^{2} = \Vert \mathbf{\text{u}} \Vert^{2} \Vert \mathbf{\text{v}} \Vert^{2} - ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} )^{2} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Verify Lagrange’s identity $$\Vert \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} \Vert^{2} = \Vert \mathbf{\text{u}} \Vert^{2} \Vert \mathbf{\text{v}} \Vert^{2} - ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} )^{2}$$ for vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \text{unicode[:-]} \mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{j}} - 2 \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = 2 \mathbf{\text{i}} - \mathbf{\text{j}} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Nonzero vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are called collinear if there exists a nonzero scalar $$\alpha$$ such that $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \alpha \mathbf{\text{u}} .$$ Show that $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are collinear if and only if $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} = \mathbf{0} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Nonzero vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are called collinear if there exists a nonzero scalar $$\alpha$$ such that $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \alpha \mathbf{\text{u}} .$$ Show that vectors $$\vec{A B}$$ and $$\vec{A C}$$ are collinear, where $$A ( 4 , 1 , 0 ) ,$$ $$B ( 6 , 5 , -2 ) ,$$ and $$C ( 5 , 3 , -1 ) .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Find the area of the parallelogram with adjacent sides $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 3 , 2 , 0 \rangle$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 0 , 2 , 1 \rangle .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Find the area of the parallelogram with adjacent sides $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Consider points $$A ( 3 , -1 , 2 ) , B ( 2 , 1 , 5 ) ,$$ and $$C ( 1 , -2 , -2 ) .$$ Find the area of parallelogram $$A B C D$$ with adjacent sides $$\vec{A B}$$ and $$\vec{A C} .$$ Find the area of triangle $$A B C .$$ Find the distance from point $$A$$ to line $$B C .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are given. Use determinant notation to find vector $$\mathbf{\text{w}}$$ orthogonal to vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} .$$
Question: Consider points $$A ( 2 , -3 , 4 ) , B ( 0 , 1 , 2 ) ,$$ and $$C ( -1 , 2 , 0 ) .$$ Find the area of parallelogram $$A B C D$$ with adjacent sides $$\vec{A B}$$ and $$\vec{A C} .$$ Find the area of triangle $$A B C .$$ Find the distance from point $$B$$ to line $$A C .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \mathbf{\text{j}} + \mathbf{\text{k}} ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{k}}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle -3 , 5 , -1 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 0 , 2 , -2 \rangle ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle 3 , 1 , 1 \rangle$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Calculate the triple scalar products $$\text{v} \cdot \left(\right. \text{u} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right)$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{w}} \cdotp ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} ) ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 1 , 1 , 1 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 7 , 6 , 9 \rangle ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle 4 , 2 , 7 \rangle .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Calculate the triple scalar products $$\mathbf{\text{w}} \cdotp ( \mathbf{\text{v}} \times \mathbf{\text{u}} )$$ and $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \textbf{w} \times \text{v} \left.\right) ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 4 , 2 , -1 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 2 , 5 , -3 \rangle ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle 9 , 5 , -10 \rangle .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Find vectors $$\mathbf{\text{a}} , \mathbf{\text{b}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{c}}$$ with a triple scalar product given by the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}1 & 2 & 3 \\ 0 & 2 & 5 \\ 8 & 9 & 2\end{matrix}\right | .$$ Determine their triple scalar product.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: The triple scalar product of vectors $$\mathbf{\text{a}} , \mathbf{\text{b}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{c}}$$ is given by the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}0 & -2 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 4 \\ 1 & -3 & 7\end{matrix}\right | .$$ Find vector $$\text{a} - \textbf{b} + \textbf{c} .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Consider the parallelepiped with edges $$O A , O B ,$$ and $$O C ,$$ where $$A ( 2 , 1 , 0 ) , B ( 1 , 2 , 0 ) ,$$ and $$C ( 0 , 1 , \alpha ) .$$ Find the real number $$\alpha > 0$$ such that the volume of the parallelepiped is $$3$$ units 3 . For $$\alpha = 1 ,$$ find the height $$h$$ from vertex $$C$$ of the parallelepiped to the plane formed by the edges $$O A$$ and $$O B$$ .

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Consider points $$A ( \alpha , 0 , 0 ) , B ( 0 , \beta , 0 ) ,$$ and $$C ( 0 , 0 , \gamma ) ,$$ with $$\alpha ,$$ $$\beta ,$$ and $$\gamma$$ positive real numbers. Determine the volume of the parallelepiped with adjacent sides $$\vec{O A} ,$$ $$\vec{O B} ,$$ and $$\vec{O C} .$$ Find the volume of the tetrahedron with vertices $$O , A , B , \text{and} C .$$ ( Hint : The volume of the tetrahedron is $$1 \text{/} 6$$ of the volume of the parallelepiped.) Find the distance from the origin to the plane determined by $$A , B , \text{and} C .$$ Sketch the parallelepiped and tetrahedron.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Let $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ be three-dimensional vectors and $$c$$ be a real number. Prove the following properties of the cross product. $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{u}} = \mathbf{0}$$ $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times ( \mathbf{\text{v}} + \mathbf{\text{w}} ) = ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} ) + ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{w}} )$$ $$c ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} ) = ( c \mathbf{\text{u}} ) \times \mathbf{\text{v}} = \mathbf{\text{u}} \times ( c \mathbf{\text{v}} )$$ $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp \mathbf{(} \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} \mathbf{) = 0}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Show that vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 1 , 0 , -8 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 0 , 1 , 6 \rangle ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle -1 , 9 , 3 \rangle$$ satisfy the following properties of the cross product. $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{u}} = \mathbf{0}$$ $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times ( \mathbf{\text{v}} + \mathbf{\text{w}} ) = ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} ) + ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{w}} )$$ $$c ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} ) = ( c \mathbf{\text{u}} ) \times \mathbf{\text{v}} = \mathbf{\text{u}} \times ( c \mathbf{\text{v}} )$$ $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp \mathbf{(} \mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}} \mathbf{) = 0}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Nonzero vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are said to be linearly dependent if one of the vectors is a linear combination of the other two. For instance, there exist two nonzero real numbers $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta$$ such that $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \alpha \mathbf{\text{u}} + \beta \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Otherwise, the vectors are called linearly independent . Show that $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are coplanar if and only if they are linear dependent.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Consider vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 1 , 4 , -7 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 2 , -1 , 4 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle 0 , -9 , 18 \rangle ,$$ and $$\textbf{p} = \langle 0 , -9 , 17 \rangle .$$ Show that $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are coplanar by using their triple scalar product Show that $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are coplanar, using the definition that there exist two nonzero real numbers $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta$$ such that $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \alpha \mathbf{\text{u}} + \beta \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Show that $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{p}}$$ are linearly independent-that is, none of the vectors is a linear combination of the other two.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Consider points $$A ( 0 , 0 , 2 ) ,$$ $$B ( 1 , 0 , 2 ) ,$$ $$C ( 1 , 1 , 2 ) ,$$ and $$D ( 0 , 1 , 2 ) .$$ Are vectors $$\vec{A B} ,$$ $$\vec{A C} ,$$ and $$\vec{A D}$$ linearly dependent (that is, one of the vectors is a linear combination of the other two)?
Answer: Yes, $$\vec{A D} = \alpha \vec{A B} + \beta \vec{A C} ,$$ where $$\alpha = -1$$ and $$\beta = 1 .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Show that vectors $$\mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{i}} - \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{j}} + \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ are linearly independent-that is, there do not exist two nonzero real numbers $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta$$ such that $$\mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{j}} + \mathbf{\text{k}} = \alpha ( \mathbf{\text{i}} + \mathbf{\text{j}} ) + \beta ( \mathbf{\text{i}} - \mathbf{\text{j}} ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Let $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle u_{1} , u_{2} \rangle$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle v_{1} , v_{2} \rangle$$ be two-dimensional vectors. The cross product of vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ is not defined. However, if the vectors are regarded as the three-dimensional vectors $$\overset{˜}{ \left ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \right ) } = \langle u_{1} , u_{2} , 0 \rangle$$ and $$\overset{˜}{ \left ( \mathbf{\text{v}} \right ) } = \langle v_{1} , v_{2} , 0 \rangle ,$$ respectively, then, in this case, we can define the cross product of $$\overset{˜}{ \left ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \right ) }$$ and $$\overset{˜}{ \left ( \mathbf{\text{v}} \right ) } .$$ In particular, in determinant notation, the cross product of $$\overset{˜}{ \left ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \right ) }$$ and $$\overset{˜}{ \left ( \mathbf{\text{v}} \right ) }$$ is given by $$\overset{˜}{ \left ( \mathbf{\text{u}} \right ) } \times \overset{˜}{ \left ( \mathbf{\text{v}} \right ) } = \left |\begin{matrix}\mathbf{\text{i}} & \mathbf{\text{j}} & \mathbf{\text{k}} \\ u_{1} & u_{2} & 0 \\ v_{1} & v_{2} & 0\end{matrix}\right | .$$ Use this result to compute $$\left(\right. \text{i} \textit{cos} \theta + \text{j} \textit{sin} \theta \left.\right) \times \left(\right. \text{i} s i n \theta - \text{j} c o s \theta \left.\right) ,$$ where $$\theta$$ is a real number.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Consider points $$P ( 2 , 1 ) ,$$ $$Q ( 4 , 2 ) ,$$ and $$R ( 1 , 2 ) .$$ Find the area of triangle $$P , Q , \text{and} R .$$ Determine the distance from point $$R$$ to the line passing through $$P \text{and} Q .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Determine a vector of magnitude $$10$$ perpendicular to the plane passing through the x -axis and point $$P ( 1 , 2 , 4 ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Determine a unit vector perpendicular to the plane passing through the z -axis and point $$A ( 3 , 1 , -2 ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Consider $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ two three-dimensional vectors. If the magnitude of the cross product vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \times \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ is $$k$$ times larger than the magnitude of vector $$\mathbf{\text{u}} ,$$ show that the magnitude of $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ is greater than or equal to $$k ,$$ where $$k$$ is a natural number.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: [T] Assume that the magnitudes of two nonzero vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ are known. The function $$f ( \theta ) = \Vert \mathbf{\text{u}} \Vert \Vert \mathbf{\text{v}} \Vert \text{sin} \theta$$ defines the magnitude of the cross product vector $$\text{u} \times \textbf{v} ,$$ where $$\theta \in [ 0 , \pi ]$$ is the angle between $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \text{and} \mathbf{\text{v}} .$$ Graph the function $$f .$$ Find the absolute minimum and maximum of function $$f .$$ Interpret the results. If $$\Vert \mathbf{\text{u}} \Vert = 5$$ and $$\Vert \mathbf{\text{v}} \Vert = 2 ,$$ find the angle between $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \text{and} \mathbf{\text{v}}$$ if the magnitude of their cross product vector is equal to $$9 .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Find all vectors $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle w_{1} , w_{2} , w_{3} \rangle$$ that satisfy the equation $$\langle 1 , 1 , 1 \rangle \times \mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle -1 , -1 , 2 \rangle .$$
Answer: $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle w_{3} - 1 , w_{3} + 1 , w_{3} \rangle ,$$ where $$w_{3}$$ is any real number

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: Solve the equation $$\mathbf{\text{w}} \times \langle 1 , 0 , -1 \rangle = \langle 3 , 0 , 3 \rangle ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle w_{1} , w_{2} , w_{3} \rangle$$ is a nonzero vector with a magnitude of $$3 .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: [T] A mechanic uses a 12-in. wrench to turn a bolt. The wrench makes a $$30 \text{°}$$ angle with the horizontal. If the mechanic applies a vertical force of $$10$$ lb on the wrench handle, what is the magnitude of the torque at point $$P$$ (see the following figure)? Express the answer in foot-pounds rounded to two decimal places.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: [T] A boy applies the brakes on a bicycle by applying a downward force of $$20$$ lb on the pedal when the 6-in. crank makes a $$40 \text{°}$$ angle with the horizontal (see the following figure). Find the torque at point $$P .$$ Express your answer in foot-pounds rounded to two decimal places.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: [T] Find the magnitude of the force that needs to be applied to the end of a 20-cm wrench located on the positive direction of the y -axis if the force is applied in the direction $$\langle 0 , 1 , -2 \rangle$$ and it produces a $$100$$ N·m torque to the bolt located at the origin.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: [T] What is the magnitude of the force required to be applied to the end of a 1-ft wrench at an angle of $$35 \text{°}$$ to produce a torque of $$20$$ N·m?

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: [T] The force vector $$\mathbf{\text{F}}$$ acting on a proton with an electric charge of $$1.6 \times 10^{-19} \text{C}$$ (in coulombs) moving in a magnetic field $$\mathbf{\text{B}}$$ where the velocity vector $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ is given by $$\text{F} = 1.6 \times \left(10\right)^{-19} \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{B} \left.\right)$$ (here, $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ is expressed in meters per second, $$\mathbf{\text{B}}$$ is in tesla [T], and $$\mathbf{\text{F}}$$ is in newtons [N]). Find the force that acts on a proton that moves in the xy -plane at velocity $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = 10^{5} \mathbf{\text{i}} + 10^{5} \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ (in meters per second) in a magnetic field given by $$\textbf{B} = 0.3 \text{j} .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: [T] The force vector $$\mathbf{\text{F}}$$ acting on a proton with an electric charge of $$1.6 \times 10^{-19} \text{C}$$ moving in a magnetic field $$\mathbf{\text{B}}$$ where the velocity vector v is given by $$\text{F} = 1.6 \times \left(10\right)^{-19} \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{B} \left.\right)$$ (here, $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ is expressed in meters per second, $$\mathbf{\text{B}}$$ in $$\text{T} ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{F}}$$ in $$\text{N} ) .$$ If the magnitude of force $$\mathbf{\text{F}}$$ acting on a proton is $$5.9 \times 10^{-17}$$ N and the proton is moving at the speed of 300 m/sec in magnetic field $$\mathbf{\text{B}}$$ of magnitude 2.4 T, find the angle between velocity vector $$\mathbf{\text{v}}$$ of the proton and magnetic field $$\mathbf{\text{B}} .$$ Express the answer in degrees rounded to the nearest integer.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: [T] Consider $$\textbf{r} \left(\right. t \left.\right) = \langle \text{cos} t , \text{sin} t , 2 t \rangle$$ the position vector of a particle at time $$t \in [ 0 , 30 ] ,$$ where the components of $$\mathbf{\text{r}}$$ are expressed in centimeters and time in seconds. Let $$\vec{O P}$$ be the position vector of the particle after $$1$$ sec. Determine unit vector $$\textbf{B} \left(\right. t \left.\right)$$ (called the binormal unit vector ) that has the direction of cross product vector $$\text{v} \left(\right. t \left.\right) \times \textbf{a} \left(\right. t \left.\right) ,$$ where $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ( t )$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{a}} ( t )$$ are the instantaneous velocity vector and, respectively, the acceleration vector of the particle after $$t$$ seconds. Use a CAS to visualize vectors $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ( 1 ) ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{a}} ( 1 ) ,$$ and $$\textbf{B} \left(\right. 1 \left.\right)$$ as vectors starting at point $$P$$ along with the path of the particle.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ are given. a) Find the triple scalar product $$\text{u} \cdot \left(\right. \text{v} \times \textbf{w} \left.\right) .$$ b) Find the volume of the parallelepiped with the adjacent edges $$\mathbf{\text{u}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{w}} .$$
Question: A solar panel is mounted on the roof of a house. The panel may be regarded as positioned at the points of coordinates (in meters) $$A ( 8 , 0 , 0 ) ,$$ $$B ( 8 , 18 , 0 ) ,$$ $$C ( 0 , 18 , 8 ) ,$$ and $$D ( 0 , 0 , 8 )$$ (see the following figure). Find vector $$\textbf{n} = \overset{\rightarrow}{A B} \times \overset{\rightarrow}{A D}$$ perpendicular to the surface of the solar panels. Express the answer using standard unit vectors. Assume unit vector $$\textbf{s} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \text{i} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \text{j} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \text{k}$$ points toward the Sun at a particular time of the day and the flow of solar energy is $$\text{F} = 900 \textbf{s}$$ (in watts per square meter [ $$\text{W/m}^{2}$$ ]). Find the predicted amount of electrical power the panel can produce, which is given by the dot product of vectors $$\mathbf{\text{F}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{n}}$$ (expressed in watts). Determine the angle of elevation of the Sun above the solar panel. Express the answer in degrees rounded to the nearest whole number. ( Hint : The angle between vectors $$\mathbf{\text{n}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{s}}$$ and the angle of elevation are complementary.)
